User Guides

Source: vignettes/articles/user_guides.Rmd
user_guides.Rmd

The admiral User Guides are grouped by topic so you can quickly find conceptual guidance, end-to-end ADaM examples, and focused methodology references.

General Guides

Programming Concepts and Conventions

Learn the core admiral programming principles, including expression handling, missing-value conventions, and common workflow expectations.

Generic Derivations

Get an overview of reusable derivation patterns and functions for adding variables, parameters, and records across many ADaM use cases.

Higher Order Functions

See how to use higher-order helpers like call_derivation(), restrict_derivation(), and slice_derivation() to reduce repetitive derivation code.

ADaM Script Guides

Creating a Basic ADSL

Follow a practical step-by-step workflow to build ADSL from SDTM sources, from treatment and disposition variables through final labeling.

Creating an OCCDS ADaM

Walk through a full OCCDS example (ADAE-focused) covering timing, treatment-emergent logic, flags, sequence derivation, and final dataset assembly.

Creating a BDS Findings ADaM

Build a BDS findings dataset (ADVS-style) with examples for parameters, baseline/change logic, analysis flags, categorization, and record creation.

Creating a BDS Exposure ADaM

Explore example approaches for structuring exposure analysis data in BDS format, including one-to-one records, summaries, and parameter mappings.

Creating a BDS Time-to-Event ADaM

Learn how to define events and censoring sources and derive core time-to-event analysis variables and parameters.

Creating Questionnaire ADaMs

Review common questionnaire derivation patterns for original items, transformed values, and score/scale parameters rather than a single fixed workflow.

Creating a PK NCA or Population PK ADaM

Work through examples for building PK analysis datasets (ADNCA/ADPC and ADPPK), with emphasis on relative timing and dose-reference derivations.

Creating an Antidrug Antibody ADaM

See an experimental ADAB workflow covering data staging, parameter computation, visit/summary assembly, and final analysis-variable derivations.

Hy’s Law Implementation

Use ADLB-based examples to derive criteria flags and joined records that support identifying potential Hy’s Law drug-induced liver injury events.

Special Topics Guides

Date and Time Imputation

Understand admiral date/time imputation rules and apply both vector-level and dataset-level imputation workflows for ADaM timing variables.

Visit and Period Variables

Learn practical strategies and helper functions for deriving visit windows plus period, subperiod, and phase variables in study-specific designs.

Queries Dataset Documentation

Reference the required structure and content of query metadata datasets used by derive_vars_query() for grouped-event derivations.

Lab Grading

Understand how admiral implements lab toxicity grading from metadata (e.g., CTCAE and DAIDS) and how to apply grading in ADLB workflows.

Estimands

Review example patterns for representing intercurrent events and implementing estimand-oriented analysis logic in admiral datasets.

FAQ

FAQ

Find quick answers to common questions about admiral goals, scope, validation expectations, and practical usage.

On this page

Developed by F. Hoffmann-La Roche AG, GlaxoSmithKline LLC, Ben Straub, Stefan Bundfuss, Matt Bearham, Arianna Cascone, Kristin Dahnert, Jeffrey Dickinson, Ross Farrugia, Fanny Gautier, Edoardo Mancini, Gordon Miller, Lina Patil, Jim Rothstein.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
